skill.name: ghe-report
skill.description: Generate detailed GitHub Elements workflow reports with metrics, health assessments, and epic-specific analysis.
skill.summary: Create metrics, health, and epic reports for GitHub Elements workflows.
skill.author: Emasoft

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, and network-reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, file-name examples, and report text. The confirmed risk is an instruction allowing writes to ~/.claude, which can affect agent configuration and should be disclosed for a community skill.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Home Directory Configuration Access
medium_findings.description: The skill permits background agents to write to ~/.claude for plugin or settings fixes. This is a real filesystem risk because that hidden configuration directory can influence future agent behavior, although no malicious write action is shown.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly names ~/.claude as an allowed write target. The risk is clear, but the surrounding text limits the purpose to plugin or settings fixes.

low_findings.title: Markdown Syntax Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code, report naming examples, fenced examples, and output templates. No evidence found of executable Ruby code or shell invocation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown text and fenced examples, not source code. The file contains no Ruby execution context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits appear to match ordinary Markdown content such as the frontmatter description, .md report names, the REQUIREMENTS directory reference, and the ghe-status name. No cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not call or describe MD5, SHA1, DES, or any cryptographic function. The matches are caused by prose and file naming.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance line describes epic report content, including current phase and progress. No evidence found of network scanning, host discovery, or probing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 88 is report output guidance and contains no network target, command, or reconnaissance workflow. The surrounding section is an epic-status template.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Configuration Directory Write Permission
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows writes to ~/.claude, a hidden agent configuration directory. Marketplace users should review this permission before enabling the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The permission is explicitly stated and applies to a sensitive hidden directory. No automatic or malicious modification is shown.

content.value_statement: GitHub Elements teams need clear reporting on throughput, compliance, and epic progress. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create structured workflow reports.
actual_capabilities: Defines metrics reports for throughput, cycle times, review rate, and demotion rate.
Defines health reports for compliance status, stale threads, violations, and synchronization.
Defines epic reports for thread history, current phase, key decisions, and remaining work.
Requires reports to be posted to both GitHub issue threads and GHE_REPORTS.
Uses local .claude/ghe.local.md settings to adjust report detail when available.
limitations: It provides reporting instructions, not a standalone reporting executable.
It depends on a separate reporter agent being available in the workspace.
It does not define GitHub API authentication or posting mechanics.
It permits writes to ~/.claude, which requires review before use.

use_cases.title: Review Team Throughput
use_cases.description: Generate a metrics report that summarizes completed features, fixed bugs, cycle times, and review quality trends.

use_cases.title: Audit Workflow Health
use_cases.description: Create a health report that checks rule compliance, stale threads, violation history, and memory bank synchronization.

use_cases.title: Analyze One Epic
use_cases.description: Produce an epic report that explains thread history, current phase, key decisions, and remaining work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Metrics Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-report to create a metrics report for the current GitHub Elements workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for standard performance reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Health Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-report to create a health report covering compliance status, stale threads, violation history, and synchronization status.

prompt_templates.title: Epic Deep Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-report to create an epic report for [epic name], including thread history, current phase, key decisions, and remaining work.
prompt_templates.scenario: Project lead needs a focused status report.

prompt_templates.title: Combined Leadership Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-report to prepare metrics and health reports, then summarize the main risks, trends, and recommended follow-up actions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced report package for leadership review.

output_examples.input: Create a metrics report for this week.
output_examples.output: A structured report with throughput, cycle time, quality metrics, and agent performance comparison.

output_examples.input: Create a health report for workflow compliance.
output_examples.output: A compliance-focused report with rule status, stale thread notes, violation history, and overall health assessment.

output_examples.input: Create an epic report for the checkout redesign.
output_examples.output: A focused epic analysis covering thread history, current phase, technical decisions, and remaining work.

best_practices: Post the full report text to both required destinations.
Use the epic report type when stakeholders need decision history.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for quick current-state summaries.
Do not delete GHE_REPORTS files unless the user explicitly requests deletion.
Do not enable ~/.claude writes without reviewing the local security impact.

faq.question: What report types does this skill define?
faq.answer: It defines metrics, health, and epic reports for GitHub Elements workflows.

faq.question: Does it run a reporting program by itself?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the assistant to spawn a reporter agent with the requested report type.

faq.question: Where should reports be posted?
faq.answer: Reports should be posted in the GitHub issue thread and saved under GHE_REPORTS.

faq.question: Can it create quick status summaries?
faq.answer: It is intended for detailed analysis. The skill text says ghe-status is better for quick overviews.

faq.question: Does it support local settings?
faq.answer: Yes. It references .claude/ghe.local.md for enabled state, notification level, and stale threshold.

faq.question: What security review is needed before use?
faq.answer: Review the permission to write to ~/.claude because that directory can affect agent configuration.
